claims.statement: Patch benchmark schema v9 and matrix schema v3 record requested compression level, package and target-entry process CPU, and the rewritten target DEFLATE writer CPU envelope; raw compressed-copy entries report zero DEFLATE writer CPU.

On this machine and the recorded one-million-cell numeric targeted-upsert workload, level 1 versus level 6 reduced median save time from 1033 ms to 488 ms and median DEFLATE writer CPU from 890625 us to 203125 us while output size grew 9.73%; level 9 reduced size only 0.11% versus level 6 while median save time rose to 9296 ms.

On the recorded mixed-inline workload, level 1 versus level 6 reduced median save time from 1244 ms to 408 ms and median DEFLATE writer CPU from 953125 us to 218750 us while output size grew 14.51%; level 9 median save time was 6735 ms.
The formula plus external-hyperlink level 1 and level 6 representative outputs opened with openpyxl 3.1.2 and preserved https://example.com/fastxlsx-benchmark.

Production and stored-only CTest passed 132/132; no-images runtime and production/stored/no-images install-consumer profiles passed. Office was not run, and no cross-machine, arbitrary-workbook, pure-encoder, backend-superiority or OpenXLSX-superiority claim is made.
